commentary_text: The model favors Australia at 44% while the market favors the draw at 42%, marking a fundamental disagreement on the match structure. The largest single gap appears on the Australia win, where the model assigns 44% but the sportsbooks offer only 24%—a 20-percentage-point spread. The market simultaneously elevates Paraguay's chances to 34% and the draw to 42%, compared to the model's 26% and 31% respectively. This inversion could reflect recent-form shifts, lineup changes, or injury news unavailable to the statistical model since its last calibration. The divergence is substantial enough that it warrants attention to real-time team information, though the dashboard reports both signals without adjudicating which proves more accurate.
